definition: EBITDA addbacks of 15-40% and covenant-lite structures (>90% of leveraged loans) create hidden leverage where true leverage is 7.5-8.5x vs reported ~6x. Covenant-lite delays default recognition by 12-24 months but increases loss-given-default, producing a 'fewer but larger' default pattern with estimated recoveries of 40-50 cents vs historical 60-70 cents.
evidence: ECB and Fed regulatory analyses document the 15-40% addback range
>90% of leveraged loans lack maintenance covenants
Default models trained on pre-2010 covenanted data are miscalibrated for current universe
Covenant-lite delays default 12-24 months but increases loss-given-default
Merton distance-to-default is significantly narrower than reported financials indicate
open_questions: What is the precise Merton distance-to-default adjustment for addback-distorted leverage?
By how much do observable credit metrics understate true cycle positioning?
How should default models be recalibrated for the covenant-lite universe?
